scholarships.name: Gates Scholarship
scholarships.description: Full cost-of-attendance scholarship for outstanding minority high school seniors with financial need. ~300 scholars selected annually from 50,000+ applicants. Covers tuition, room, board, books, transportation, and personal expenses not covered by other aid.

scholarships.name: QuestBridge National College Match
scholarships.description: Matches high-achieving, low-income seniors with full four-year scholarships at 50+ top partner colleges including MIT, Stanford, Yale, Princeton, Columbia, and more. Covers tuition, room, board, books, and travel.

scholarships.name: Coca-Cola Scholars Program
scholarships.description: 150 scholars selected from 107,000+ applicants. $20,000 each. Recognizes leadership, service, and impact on school/community. Three-phase process: application, semifinalist essays, finalist interviews.

scholarships.name: Elks Most Valuable Student Scholarship
scholarships.description: 500 four-year scholarships awarded annually. Top 20 win $30,000; 480 runners-up receive $4,000. Judged on scholarship, leadership, and financial need. Leadership Weekend in Chicago for finalists.

scholarships.name: Jack Kent Cooke Foundation Scholarship
scholarships.description: Competitive full-ride for high-achieving, low-income students combining merit and financial need. Judges seek intellectual curiosity, maturity, and demonstrated ability to thrive in rigorous academic environments. The foundation values students who will become leaders and engaged citizens.
scholarships.strategyTip: Show intellectual engagement beyond coursework—independent reading, projects, self-directed learning. Demonstrate maturity in your writing and ability to reflect on your growth and values.

scholarships.name: Dell Scholars Program
scholarships.description: 500 scholars selected annually. $20,000 + Dell laptop + textbook credits + ongoing mentoring. Must be Pell Grant-eligible and have participated in an approved college-readiness program (AVID, Upward Bound, GEAR UP, etc.).

scholarships.name: Posse Foundation Scholarship
scholarships.description: Full-ride program placing teams (posses) of diverse students in selective colleges with dedicated mentorship and support. Selection emphasizes leadership potential, interpersonal skills, and diversity of background and perspective. Collaboration and commitment to lifting peers are essential values.
scholarships.strategyTip: Posse prioritizes collaborative leaders, not solo achievers. Show how you build teams, resolve conflicts, and lift others up. The interview process is rigorous—practice discussing diversity and leadership.

scholarships.name: Barry Goldwater Scholarship
scholarships.description: Prestigious mathematics and science scholarship recognizing students with exceptional potential for research careers in STEM. Selections committee values demonstrated research aptitude, intellectual independence, and passion for advancing knowledge. Your application must show serious engagement with advanced science or mathematics.
scholarships.strategyTip: Include research experience or independent projects—even if informal. Show how you think like a scientist: identifying questions, designing solutions, and iterating on your understanding.

scholarships.name: Jack Kent Cooke College Scholarship
scholarships.description: Up to $55,000/year for high-achieving seniors with financial need. Requires unweighted GPA of 3.75+, SAT/ACT/AP/IB scores, and family AGI up to $95,000. One of the largest private scholarships in the country.

scholarships.name: Cameron Impact Scholarship
scholarships.description: Full tuition scholarship covering tuition, fees, and books at any accredited 4-year institution. ~25 scholars selected from 3,000 applicants. Finalists receive in-person interviews. Application closes at 3,000 submissions or May 1, whichever comes first.

scholarships.name: Horatio Alger National Scholarship
scholarships.description: For seniors who have overcome significant adversity. 600+ scholarships awarded annually ($10K-$50K). Minimum 2.0 GPA. Emphasizes perseverance, integrity, and determination. State scholarships ($10K) also available in every state.

scholarships.name: Edison Scholars
scholarships.description: 30 high school seniors each receive $50,000 for pursuing STEM degrees. Must live in Edison International's service territory (Southern California). Strong emphasis on community involvement and STEM passion.

scholarships.name: Regeneron Science Talent Search
scholarships.description: Nation's oldest and most prestigious science research competition. Top 300 scholars get $2,000 each; 40 finalists get $25,000+; top winner gets $250,000. Requires original STEM research project. $3.1M awarded annually.

scholarships.name: Davidson Fellows Scholarship
scholarships.description: Awards $25K, $50K, or $100K to students 18 and under who have completed a significant piece of work in STEM, humanities (music, literature, philosophy), or 'outside the box.' Requires graduate-level achievement.

scholarships.name: Scholarship America National STEM Excellence
scholarships.description: One-time $10,000 scholarship for future STEM leaders. Seeks students with strong academic records and demonstrated interest in science, technology, engineering, or mathematics.

scholarships.name: Let Grow Think for Yourself Scholarship
scholarships.description: $14,000 in total scholarships. 600-800 word essay on independent thinking. Open to all high school seniors. Lower competition than many essay scholarships due to specific prompt requirements.

scholarships.name: BigFuture Scholarships (College Board)
scholarships.description: Complete college planning steps on BigFuture to earn scholarship entries. $500 and $40,000 awards. No essay required — just complete activities like building a college list and exploring majors. Open to Class of 2026.

scholarships.name: C-SPAN StudentCam Documentary Competition
scholarships.description: Create a 5-6 minute video documentary on an annual theme. 150 prizes totaling $100,000. Middle and high school categories. Grand prize $5,000. Open to grades 6-12, individuals or teams of 2-3.

scholarships.name: Project Yellow Light (Distracted Driving PSA)
scholarships.description: Create a PSA video about distracted driving awareness. $2,000 scholarship. Billboard, radio, and digital categories available. Strong social impact focus.

scholarships.name: Unboxing Your Life Video Scholarship
scholarships.description: Create a creative 5-minute video 'unboxing' your life story. $3,000 scholarship. Open to high school seniors, undergrads, and grad students. Winner announced May 18, 2026.

scholarships.name: Prudential Spirit of Community Awards
scholarships.description: Recognizes youth volunteer service. State-level honorees receive $1,000; national honorees receive $5,000 + trip to Washington DC. Two honorees per state + DC. Open to grades 5-12.

scholarships.name: Warrick Dunn Hearts for Community Scholarship
scholarships.description: $5,000 scholarship recognizing high school seniors who have demonstrated a commitment to community service, volunteering, and making a positive impact in their communities.

scholarships.name: APIA Scholars (Asian & Pacific Islander)
scholarships.description: Awards ranging from one-time $2,500 to multi-year $20,000. For Asian and Pacific Islander American students. One of the largest scholarship providers for AAPI community.

scholarships.name: NSHSS First Generation Scholarship
scholarships.provider: National Society of High School Scholars
scholarships.description: $2,500 for first-generation college students who are NSHSS members. Recognizes academic achievement and community involvement among students who will be the first in their family to attend college.

scholarships.name: Washington State Opportunity Scholarship (WSOS) — Baccalaureate
scholarships.description: Up to $22,500 for WA residents pursuing high-demand STEM and healthcare majors. Must complete FAFSA/WASFA. Family income at or below 125% of WA median. Includes career-launching support services.

scholarships.name: Washington College Grant

amount.display: Up to full tuition at public institutions

scholarships.description: Washington's largest state financial aid program. Covers tuition and some fees at public colleges/universities for eligible low-income residents. Complete FAFSA or WASFA to apply. No separate application needed.

scholarships.name: College Bound Scholarship (Washington)

amount.display: Tuition at state's highest-priced public university

scholarships.description: Sign up in 7th or 8th grade, maintain 2.0 GPA, stay out of trouble, and file FAFSA/WASFA as a senior. Covers tuition and a book allowance. Must sign the College Bound pledge by June 30 of 8th grade.

scholarships.name: Seattle Foundation Scholarships
scholarships.description: Multiple scholarship funds administered through Seattle Foundation for WA students. Various criteria including financial need, field of study, and community involvement. One application covers multiple funds.

scholarships.name: WAVE (Washington Award for Vocational Excellence)
scholarships.description: Recognizes graduating seniors for outstanding achievement in career and technical education (CTE). Tuition waiver at WA public colleges. Nominated by school CTE programs.

scholarships.name: NYS STEM Incentive Program
scholarships.description: Full tuition scholarship at SUNY or CUNY for NY high school graduates ranked in top 10% of their class pursuing STEM degrees. Must work in STEM field in NY for 5 years after graduation.

scholarships.name: Horatio Alger State Scholarship — Texas
scholarships.description: $10,000 for Texas high school juniors who have overcome significant adversity and demonstrate financial need. Part of the Horatio Alger state scholarship network available in all 50 states.

scholarships.name: Horatio Alger State Scholarship — California
scholarships.description: $10,000 for California high school juniors who have overcome significant adversity and demonstrate financial need.

scholarships.name: Horatio Alger State Scholarship — Michigan
scholarships.description: $10,000 for Michigan high school juniors who have overcome significant adversity and demonstrate financial need.

scholarships.name: Horatio Alger State Scholarship — New York
scholarships.description: $10,000 for New York high school juniors who have overcome significant adversity and demonstrate financial need.

scholarships.name: Horatio Alger State Scholarship — Washington
scholarships.description: $10,000 for Washington high school juniors who have overcome significant adversity and demonstrate financial need.

scholarships.name: College Success Foundation Leadership 1000 Scholarship
scholarships.description: Renewable scholarship for WA state high school seniors with at least 2.5 GPA and financial need. Must plan to attend eligible WA state college/university pursuing 4-year degree. Scholars also receive career and financial aid guidance plus networking with alumni.

scholarships.name: Washington CPA Foundation Accounting Scholarship
scholarships.description: Over 20 scholarships of $2,000 each for WA state high school seniors interested in accounting careers. Must plan to attend a WA state college or university. The WA CPA Foundation awarded over $650,000 in scholarships in 2025-2026.

scholarships.name: American Indian Endowed Scholarship (Washington)
scholarships.description: For students with close social and cultural ties to an American Indian tribe or community in Washington State. Must demonstrate financial need and plan to attend an eligible WA institution.

scholarships.name: Science Ambassador Scholarship
scholarships.description: Full-tuition scholarship for women and non-binary students in STEM. Applicants submit a 3-minute educational video teaching a STEM topic. Five winners receive $20,000 each. Funded by Cards Against Humanity. No GPA requirement.

scholarships.name: Fountainhead Essay Contest
scholarships.description: Write an 800-1,600 word essay on Ayn Rand's The Fountainhead. Open to all middle and high school students worldwide age 13+. First prize $25,000, multiple runner-up prizes. One of the largest essay contests in the US.

scholarships.name: Freedom From Religion Foundation Essay Contest
scholarships.description: Annual essay contest for high school students on topics related to separation of church and state or personal experiences as a freethinker. Multiple prize levels from $200 to $3,500.

scholarships.name: Ocean Awareness Student Contest
scholarships.description: Students explore ocean topics through visual art, creative writing, poetry, spoken word, music, dance, or film. Open to students ages 11-18 worldwide. Multiple award categories and cash prizes.

scholarships.name: YoungArts Foundation Awards
scholarships.description: Prestigious national arts competition in 10 disciplines including visual arts, film, music, dance, theater, writing, and more. Winners receive cash awards up to $10,000 plus masterclasses and national recognition. Gateway to Presidential Scholars in the Arts.

scholarships.name: AXA Achievement Scholarship
scholarships.description: Recognizes outstanding achievement in school, community, or workplace. 52 state winners each get $2,500; national winners get up to $25,000. Emphasizes non-traditional community engagement and self-driven projects.

scholarships.name: Edison Scholars Program (Southern California)
scholarships.description: Up to 30 awards of $12,500/year ($50K total over 4 years) for HS seniors in SCE service area pursuing STEM degrees. Must demonstrate financial need and 3.0+ GPA.

scholarships.name: Buick Achievers Scholarship Program
scholarships.description: Renewable scholarship up to $25,000/year for students planning to major in STEM fields. ~50 scholars selected annually. Special consideration for women, minorities, military veterans, and first-generation college students. Funded by the GM Foundation.

scholarships.name: Carol S. Comeau Environmental Scholarship
scholarships.provider: Carol S. Comeau Environmental Scholarship Fund
scholarships.description: Scholarship for Washington state public high school seniors planning to pursue a degree in environmental studies. Four winners selected annually. Essay-based application about how you hope to make a difference through environmental studies.

scholarships.name: Cooke Young Scholars Program

amount.display: Comprehensive support through high school + pathway to $55K/yr college scholarship

scholarships.description: Selective five-year pre-college scholarship for exceptional 7th graders with financial need. Provides individualized academic advising, financial support for enrichment, and a pathway to the Cooke College Scholarship ($55K/year). Family AGI up to $95,000 eligible.

scholarships.name: Washington State College Grant

amount.display: Up to full tuition at public institutions

scholarships.description: Washington's largest state financial aid program, covering up to the full cost of tuition at public colleges. Available to Washington residents with family income at or below 100% of state median family income (~$70K for family of four). Apply via FAFSA or WASFA.

scholarships.name: NSHSS Foundation STEM Scholarship
scholarships.provider: National Society of High School Scholars
scholarships.description: Scholarship supporting students with demonstrated interest in STEM fields. Applicants need resume, transcript, educator recommendation, and essay about why they want to study STEM and how they will give back to their community.

scholarships.name: SAME Seattle Post & PSE&SSF Engineering Scholarship
scholarships.provider: Society of American Military Engineers — Seattle Post
scholarships.description: Up to $27,500 awarded across 18 scholarships for graduating high school seniors in Puget Sound counties pursuing BS degrees in engineering, science, or technology. Awards range from $1,000-$2,000. Must attend an accredited college or university in Fall 2026.

scholarships.name: Museum of Flight Post-Secondary STEM Scholarship
scholarships.description: Renewable STEM scholarship for Washington State resident high school seniors with 3.5+ GPA who participated in at least 40 hours of Museum of Flight programs. Awards up to $30,000 annually for four years toward a STEM degree.

scholarships.name: Nellie Martin Carman Scholarship
scholarships.description: Founded in 1949. For graduating seniors from public high schools in King, Pierce, or Snohomish County, WA. Must demonstrate financial need. Requires school counselor nomination. Must attend a WA college or university full-time. Not available for arts majors.

scholarships.name: Black at Microsoft Scholarship
scholarships.description: National scholarship for Black high school seniors pursuing STEM or business degrees. Five $5,000 renewable awards (up to 3 years) and 55 one-time $2,500 awards. Administered by Scholarship America. Selection based on academics, interest in software/tech, and leadership.

scholarships.name: WWIN Star Scholarship
scholarships.description: Annual $5,000 renewable scholarship for women in Washington state pursuing post-secondary credentials. Renewable up to $20,000 total. Includes a Resiliency Fund for financial setbacks, Career Coaching program, and online networking community. Open to US citizens, permanent residents, refugees, asylees, and DACA recipients.

scholarships.name: Pride Foundation Scholarships
scholarships.description: 60+ scholarships for LGBTQ+ students and allies in the Pacific Northwest (AK, ID, MT, OR, WA). One application covers all awards. Over $8.5 million awarded to 2,400+ students. Average award is $6,700. Renewable — students can reapply annually.

scholarships.name: We The Future Contest
scholarships.description: Submit an essay (500-1000 words), song, STEM project, short film (5-10 min), social media video (60 sec), or PSA (~30 sec) on the U.S. Constitution. Open to elementary through college. High school winners may receive an additional $5,000 Hillsdale College scholarship.

scholarships.name: Stossel in the Classroom Essay Contest
scholarships.description: Essay contest for grades 5-12 requiring a 500-1000 word original essay. For 2026, the topic focuses on America's 250th anniversary milestone. Finalists must participate in a brief interview. Open to students in the US, Canada, or at US military addresses.

scholarships.name: College JumpStart Scholarship
scholarships.description: Merit-based scholarship requiring a personal statement about educational goals. No GPA or test score requirements. Multiple scholarship tracks with different themes and deadlines throughout the year (Show Grit, Gratitude, Pay It Forward, Love of Learning).

scholarships.name: Terry Foundation Scholarship
scholarships.description: Full-ride, last-dollar scholarship covering up to 8 semesters at 11 Texas public universities including UT Austin, Texas A&M, UH, and Texas Tech. Covers tuition, room, board, books, and includes a $5,000 study abroad stipend. Selected based on academic achievement, leadership, and financial need.
